mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations. A month-over-month drop in FEDFUNDS predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between month-over-month FEDFUNDS changes and 20-day forward returns remains below 0.02 across all rolling 5-year windows, or the signal produces no statistically significant alpha.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 7.5 percent, labor market slack suppresses wage growth and corporate profit margins. This threshold breach predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings revisions turn downward.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 60-day returns in more than 65 percent of months where UNRATE remains above 7.5 percent, or the out-of-sample hit rate falls below 50 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real sales of automobiles and housing starts signals broad-based consumer demand recovery. The joint positive momentum of RSAFS and HOUST predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as cyclical earnings accelerate.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline market-neutral strategy, with cumulative alpha below 0.5 percent per month over a 3-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary easing cycle, rising industrial production indicates capacity utilization is catching up to demand without triggering inflation fears. Month-over-month increases in INDPRO predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The positive relationship between INDPRO momentum and 5-day equity returns reverses or becomes statistically insignificant when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects stable economic activity and consumer spending capacity. A positive month-over-month change in PAYEMS predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between PAYEMS monthly changes and 20-day forward returns remains below 0.04 across all rolling 5-year windows, or the signal shows zero predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index breaches 230, inflation expectations rise sharply, forcing markets to price in tighter monetary policy. This threshold crossing predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days due to higher discount rates.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in more than 60 percent of observations where CPIAUCSL exceeds 230, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance.

mechanism_narrative: Strong quarterly GDP growth combined with persistently low federal funds rates creates a favorable earnings environment. The interaction of high GDPC1 growth and FEDFUNDS below 0.5 percent predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative cumulative returns over a 2-year out-of-sample period, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: When the index of industrial production falls below 95, it signals a contraction in manufacturing output and weak business investment. This threshold breach predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as risk sentiment deteriorates.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns, with a hit rate below 45 percent over a 3-year walk-forward test, or the relationship vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS.
